destinations.evidence: Candidate block-size accounting uses checked additions and maps overflow to usize::MAX, which is then rejected by the configured/Osaka size-limit checks rather than wrapping.

equivalence_argument: The old can_fit_transaction fail-closed behavior is preserved structurally by the shared estimated_block_size_with_candidate helper: any addition overflow becomes usize::MAX, so size-limit predicates reject the candidate.
notes: The implementation name changed from a boolean helper to an estimated-size helper, but the rejection behavior is equivalent.
rationale: The can_fit_transaction helper now uses current_size.checked_add(tx_size).map(...).unwrap_or(false), preventing overflow from making an oversized transaction appear to fit. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Block size fit checks should use checked addition and return false on overflow instead of wrapping current_size + tx_size.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set current_size near usize::MAX and tx_size large enough to overflow; assert can_fit_transaction returns false for a configured max block size rather than wrapping and returning true.
summary: Block-size fit checks fail closed on overflow.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set current RLP length near usize::MAX with a block-size limit active and assert the candidate is skipped because the estimate saturates to usize::MAX.
title: Block size fit checks fail closed on overflow
